name: United Kingdom
plain_words: Tax in the UK: VAT on what you sell, Corporation Tax on profits, PAYE on payroll, Self Assessment for the self-employed. File on time, keep digital records (MTD), and remember: prepared means ready, filed means sent.

taxes.name: VAT
taxes.plain_words: VAT is a tax on what you sell. You charge 20% extra, hold it for HMRC, send it quarterly. Register when sales pass £90,000/year.
taxes.notes: Making Tax Digital requires digital records and quarterly updates

deadlines.name: quarterly_return
deadlines.detail: File by 7th of the second month after quarter ends

taxes.name: Corporation Tax
taxes.plain_words: Tax on company profits. Main rate 25%, small profits rate 19%. File CT600 within 12 months of year end.
taxes.notes: Marginal relief applies between small profits and main rate

deadlines.name: ct600_filing
deadlines.detail: File CT600 within 12 months of accounting period end

taxes.name: PAYE
taxes.plain_words: Pay As You Earn — tax on employees' wages, reported in real time (RTI). FPS before each payday, EPS by 19th of following month.
taxes.notes: RTI requires real-time reporting via FPS and EPS

deadlines.name: fps
deadlines.detail: Full Payment Submission on or before each payday

deadlines.name: eps
deadlines.detail: Employer Payment Summary by 19th of following month

taxes.name: Self Assessment
taxes.plain_words: For self-employed and others with untaxed income. File SA100 online by 31 January. Pay tax by same date.
taxes.notes: Two payment dates: 31 Jan (balancing payment + first payment on account), 31 Jul (second payment on account)

deadlines.name: sa100_online
deadlines.detail: File online by 31 January after tax year ends
deadlines.penalty: £100 day 1, then £10/day for 90 days, then percentage-based

deadlines.name: payment_1
deadlines.detail: Balancing payment + first payment on account
